domain: Mongolian airag continuous-feed fermentation (started once at season begin; daily addition of fresh milk + thousands of stirs; never reset; distributed stirring by all family members; annual seasonal cycle)
specific_mechanism: Always-on slow continuous fermentation: small daily input additions + many micro-mixing iterations; never reset; distributed maintenance; long-running stable evolving state.
llm_application: AIRAG-FERMENT: an always-on continuous fine-tuning regime where (i) single base checkpoint initialized once per long cycle (e.g., quarter or year); (ii) every day a small batch of fresh data is appended to a persistent training stream; (iii) many tiny gradient steps integrate it (no batch-mode large epoch); (iv) NO FULL RESET within the cycle; (v) distributed stirring = federated micro-updates from multiple worker shards each contributing small gradient deltas continuously; (vi) annual refresh re-initializes from scratch. Different from one-shot fine-tuning, continual learning replay (which has resets/replays), and RLHF (episodic) — this is always-on slow incremental ferment.
novelty_claim: WSD scheduling has warmup-stable-decay phases (single training session). Continual pretraining (rewarm-then-train) does periodic restarts. RLHF is episodic. Online learning has per-sample updates but typically without distributed-stirring + annual-cycle structure. AIRAG-FERMENT differs by ALWAYS-ON CONTINUOUS-FEED WITHOUT RESET + DISTRIBUTED MICRO-STIRRING + LONG ANNUAL CYCLE STRUCTURE — explicit long-cycle continuous-feed regime.
